summary: Large assembly-only cleanup aligning many unmatched or partially matched .data sections with `.balign 8`. The PR body explicitly says it "Does work of `.balign 8` on files that weren't in C or partially matched for .data only." The diff shows repeated insertion or replacement of alignment directives immediately after `.section .data` / `.section .data, "wa"` across MSL, MetroTRK, Dolphin SDK, sysdolphin baselib, and many Melee subsystems, with a few padding/null cleanup edits where alignment made manual padding unnecessary.
what_changed: Added `.balign 8` at the start of `.data` sections in many assembly files that were not yet translated to C or were only partially matched.
Replaced some existing weaker alignment such as `.balign 4` with `.balign 8`, for example in `asm/MSL/printf.s` before `lbl_80400430`.
Applied the same pattern to `.section .data, "wa"` in `asm/MSL/trigf.s`, adding `.balign 8` before `lbl_80400770`.
Touched 216 files with small line-count changes, mostly one-line alignment insertions in `.data` blocks.
Covered library/runtime areas including MSL, MetroTRK, Dolphin card/dsp/dvd/gx/os/pad/vi, and many Melee systems such as camera, effects, fighter, game, ground/stage, and sysdolphin baselib.
Removed at least one apparent manual trailing padding word in `asm/melee/cm/camera.s` (`.4byte NULL`) after aligning the data section, indicating some hand padding became unnecessary or incorrect.
Some files had minor formatting-only spacing around sections alongside the alignment addition, as seen in `asm/MSL/trigf.s`.
smart_moves: Handled alignment as a broad mechanical pass rather than fixing individual data-address drift one file at a time.
Placed `.balign 8` immediately after the `.section .data` declaration, making section layout intent obvious and consistent.
Limited the PR to `.data` alignment for asm/unmatched or partially matched files, avoiding unrelated code decompilation or behavioral changes.
Standardized alignment across SDK, runtime, engine, and gameplay assembly files, which reduces future surprises when files are converted to C.
Removed redundant manual padding where alignment directives better express the desired layout.
decomp_lessons: For Melee assembly slices that still own `.data`, the section should often begin with `.balign 8`; missing this can shift every following data symbol and hurt matching.
Prefer declarative section alignment (`.balign 8`) over ad hoc `.4byte NULL` padding when the goal is section-start or symbol alignment.
Data-section layout cleanup can be safely separated from function matching work; a dedicated alignment-only PR is easier to audit.
When converting asm to C or partially matching a file, verify not only function text but also the alignment of the file's emitted `.data` block.
Broad alignment inconsistencies in generated or hand-maintained asm can mask true data-structure issues; normalize alignment before inferring struct fields from address drift.
naming_conventions: No new semantic names were introduced; existing temporary labels such as `lbl_80400430`, `jtbl_80400810`, and file-local `func_...`/`lbl_...` symbols were preserved.

The diff retains existing subsystem file naming conventions, including SDK-style uppercase filenames such as `OSAlloc.s`, `GXAttr.s`, and Melee lowercase module names such as `fighter.s`, `gmmain.s`, and `grbattle.s`.
Jump tables remain named with the existing `jtbl_` prefix where already present, for example `jtbl_80400810` and `jtbl_80401048`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Insert `    .balign 8` directly below `.section .data` or `.section .data, "wa"` before the first `.global` data symbol.
Replace initial `.balign 4` with `.balign 8` when the section needs 8-byte alignment.
After adding section-level alignment, re-check nearby manual null padding because some padding words may become unnecessary or produce wrong layout.

Use this as a pre-C-migration cleanup tactic for files that are still fully asm or partially matched, especially when `.data` contains strings, function pointer tables, jump tables, floats, or SDK global data.
Keep the change mechanical and localized to data directives to preserve text-section matching.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal review notes were present in the provided PR context.
The PR was merged immediately after creation, so there is no recorded reviewer rationale beyond the title/body and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove `.balign 8` from asm files when converting the corresponding `.data` to C.
Search for remaining `.section .data` blocks without `.balign 8` in unmatched assembly files.
Check whether any manual `.4byte NULL` padding remains immediately before `.bss` after section alignment passes.
Compare current generated asm conventions for `.data, "wa"` sections against this PR's `.balign 8` placement.
Look for future reviews discussing when `.balign 8` versus `.balign 4` is correct for Melee data symbols.
